title: Item
description: A self-describing item — a superset of every property an item MIGHT have. Only `name` is required; a torch has no damage dice, a sword has no AC, so most fields are simply absent. The same contract is used for equipped items (by slot on the sheet) and for backpack lines (with a quantity). Content-neutral: item/category/property/type names are catalog-driven, never enumerated here. Magic-item effects are deliberately NOT modelled yet — they will slot in later via a new `effects` field without restructuring.

category.description: Item category identifier (catalog), e.g. weapon / armor / shield / gear.

quantity.description: How many the character holds. Used by the backpack, and also on an equipped stack (thrown weapons, loaded ammunition).

rarity.description: Rarity identifier (catalog); null for mundane items.

attunement.description: Attunement state for a magic item. Absent for items that don't use attunement.

properties.description: Item/weapon property identifiers (catalog), e.g. two-handed / versatile / light / finesse.

versatile_damage.description: Weapon: damage dice when wielded two-handed (versatile weapons), e.g. '1d10'.

range.description: Weapon: range, e.g. '30/120' or null for melee-only.

grip.description: Weapon: how it is currently held (catalog) — one-handed / two-handed. Selects versatile_damage and governs shield/two-handed exclusivity. Null / absent when not held.

ammunition.description: Weapon: the ammunition this weapon consumes and how much is currently loaded/on hand. Absent for weapons that need none.

charges.description: Item: an expendable charge economy (wands, staves, some wondrous items). Absent for items without charges.

recharge.description: Recharge trigger identifier (catalog), e.g. a dawn/short-rest token.

effects.description: Activated powers this item grants (e.g. 'Produce Liquid' on Alchemy Jug). NOT validated — these are documentation for item-specific activated abilities.

armor_category.description: Armour: category identifier (catalog) — light / medium / heavy.

base_ac.description: Armour: base AC before the Dexterity modifier.

dex_cap.description: Armour: max Dex bonus to AC — null = uncapped (light), 2 (medium). Heavy armour adds no Dexterity at all (not even a negative modifier): use 0, meaning the applied Dex contribution is clamped to the 0..cap range.

ac_bonus.description: Shield (or AC-granting item): bonus to AC.
